lastUpdatedReason: Add 5 multi-subject profiles (multi-math-strong, multi-average, multi-strong-math-weak-ela, multi-history-focus, multi-all-subjects). Update targets from 24 to 29 profile expectations.

mastery_convergence.name: Mastery Convergence
mastery_convergence.description: Non-struggling profiles should reach ≥50% mastery by session 30, demonstrating the system drives genuine learning progression
mastery_convergence.science_ref: Bloom 1984 (2-sigma problem); Kulik et al. 1990 (~0.5-1.0 SD effect for mastery learning)
mastery_convergence.rationale: ≥11 of 19 non-struggling profiles (~58%) reaching 50% mastery in 30 sessions. Excludes struggling-young, struggling-older, struggling-middle, adult-remedial, and slow-steady. Usage-habit profiles (weekend-warrior, fading-motivation) may converge slower due to scheduling gaps. Tolerance of 2 accounts for stochastic variance.

mastery_preservation.name: Mastery Preservation
mastery_preservation.description: Diagnostic-established mastery should be retained through the first learning session without significant loss
mastery_preservation.science_ref: Mastery learning requires stability (Bloom 1984); hysteresis prevents careless-error resets
mastery_preservation.rationale: ≤10 percentage point drop from session 0 to session 1. Diagnostic places students with mastered topics; losing >10% indicates broken hysteresis or overly aggressive review criteria.

difficulty_targeting.name: 85% Difficulty Targeting
difficulty_targeting.description: Rolling problem accuracy should converge to the [0.80, 0.90] optimal learning zone for most profiles
difficulty_targeting.science_ref: Wilson et al. 2019 (Nature Communications): optimal learning at ~85% success rate
difficulty_targeting.rationale: ≥17 of 24 profiles converging within 500 problems. Strong/gifted profiles may exceed 90% (content ceiling). Struggling and usage-habit profiles may take longer. Tolerance of ±3 accounts for stochastic variance across 24 profiles.

review_new_balance.name: Review/New Balance
review_new_balance.description: Session mix should maintain ~60% review / ~40% new content to balance retention with progression
review_new_balance.science_ref: Math Academy design (~60/40 review/new); session design research on review fatigue prevention
review_new_balance.rationale: Averaged across sessions 2+ (skip session 1 warmup). Range [0.50, 0.70] with center at 0.60. Above 0.70 indicates review queue dominance; below 0.50 indicates insufficient retention practice.

interleaving.name: Interleaving Quality
interleaving.description: Topics should be mixed across strands to maximize retention through interleaved practice
interleaving.science_ref: Rohrer 2012: 76% better retention at 1-month delay; Taylor & Rohrer 2010: doubled test scores with interleaving
interleaving.rationale: Same-strand adjacency ≤10% at topic-transition level. Two profiles (misconception-fractions, struggling-older) may exceed due to prerequisite-chain remediation in one strand — this is pedagogically correct.

fire_compression.name: FIRe Review Efficiency
fire_compression.description: Encompassing-based implicit repetition should achieve mastery with fewer reviews per topic by crediting prerequisite practice
fire_compression.science_ref: Skycak 2026 (Math Academy FIRe model); Ausubel et al. 1957 (retroactive facilitation restores memory as effectively as direct repetition)
fire_compression.rationale: Break-even (0%) reviews-per-mastered-topic with vs without encompassing edges. Tolerance ±30% reflects butterfly effects at 15 sessions — removing encompassing edges changes both FIRe credit AND review ordering (set-cover optimization), causing large variance. Baseline at L2 (15 sessions): -25% average (FIRe not yet beneficial). Expected to improve at L3+ (90 sessions) where stability compounding matters. Old metric (total review count) punished FIRe for accelerating progression.

remediation_routing.name: Remediation Routing
remediation_routing.description: Students with misconceptions should receive targeted prerequisite remediation when struggling
remediation_routing.science_ref: Targeted remediation is core to mastery learning (Bloom 1984); key prerequisite repair enables progress
remediation_routing.rationale: ≥5 remediation events for misconception profiles in 15 sessions. The misconception-fractions profile has 9 fraction topics with 25-40% accuracy, so the system should detect and remediate. Low count indicates broken trigger logic.

presentation_drift.name: Presentation Drift
presentation_drift.description: Presentation level should adapt toward the appropriate level based on student performance and stabilize
presentation_drift.science_ref: Sweller et al. 2003 (expertise reversal effect): scaffolding that helps novices actively hinders experts
presentation_drift.rationale: ≥14 of 24 profiles drift in expected direction AND stabilize. Usage-habit profiles with gaps may show less stable drift due to fewer data points. Tolerance of 3 accounts for scheduling-related instability.

diagnostic_placement.name: Diagnostic Placement Accuracy
diagnostic_placement.description: Adaptive diagnostic should place all profiles within ±1 grade of their expected frontier
diagnostic_placement.science_ref: Zou et al. 2019: students 3-4x more likely to master frontier topics; adaptive testing literature
diagnostic_placement.rationale: 24 of 24 profiles within ±1 grade. Zero tolerance because misplacement by ≥2 grades wastes student time on content that is too easy or too hard. Scheduling habits don't affect diagnostic (runs once at start).

cognitive_demand_entropy.name: Cognitive Demand Entropy
cognitive_demand_entropy.description: Problems should exercise diverse cognitive demands (recall, procedural, conceptual, analytical) for transfer
cognitive_demand_entropy.science_ref: Bjork & Bjork 2011: varied practice (desirable difficulties) promotes transfer
cognitive_demand_entropy.rationale: Shannon entropy ≥0.90 bits averaged across profiles. With 4 demand types, max entropy is 2.0 bits (uniform). Current content skews toward procedural/recall demands (bridge signal — simulations approximate, live data confirms). 0.90 bits is achievable with existing content mix. Relaxed from 1.2 until content pipeline generates more balanced demand distribution.

average-older.description: Age 12, moderate ability through grade 3, tests typical older learner progression path
average-older.behavioral_signature: Should be placed at grade 3 frontier, steadily master K-3 topics, struggle at grade 4+. Presentation stays at standard (age-appropriate). Moderate remediation when encountering grade 4 content.

average-young.description: Age 7, moderate ability through grade 1, tests young learner with limited content range
average-young.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 1 frontier. Slow mastery growth limited by content ceiling and young-learner response speed. Primary presentation. Moderate remediation on grade 2 content.

fast-learner.description: Age 8, starts average but ability increases +0.5% per session, tests learning gain adaptation
fast-learner.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 2 frontier initially. Should show accelerating mastery as ability curve shifts up. Difficulty targeting should track the improving ability. Eventually reaches topics previously too hard.

misconception-fractions.description: Age 10, strong on whole-number ops but collapses on fractions, tests remediation routing
misconception-fractions.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 5 frontier (high general ability). Rapid mastery of non-fraction topics. Heavy remediation on fraction topics (9 misconception targets at 25-40%). Should trigger prerequisite-based remediation chains. High interleaving due to strand-diverse mastery.

overconfident.description: Age 10, average ability with consistently high confidence regardless of correctness
overconfident.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 2-3 frontier. Average mastery progression. Confidence-accuracy mismatch should be detectable (high confidence + wrong answers). Moderate remediation. Fastest convergence on difficulty targeting due to consistent behavior.

strong-older.description: Age 14, high ability (90%+) across all grades, tests ceiling effects and efficiency
strong-older.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 5 frontier (max). Near-100% mastery from diagnostic. Minimal or no mastery growth needed. No remediation. Difficulty targeting may exceed 90% (content ceiling). Sessions complete quickly due to content ceiling — final mastery may be lower than ability suggests. Presentation may drift to advanced due to high ability.

strong-young.description: Age 6, high ability K-2 but drops sharply at grade 3+, tests young gifted learner
strong-young.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 2 frontier. Quick mastery of K-2 topics. Heavy remediation when system pushes into grade 3 content (55% accuracy). Presentation stays primary (age 6). Known edge case: high remediation generates failure signals that may push presentation down.

struggling-older.description: Age 13, low ability even at grade 0-1, tests system behavior with below-grade-level older student
struggling-older.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 0-1 frontier. Very slow mastery growth. Heavy remediation. Difficulty targeting takes longest to converge (high oscillation at ~60% accuracy). Presentation starts standard (age) but may drift down. Tests floor behavior and patience of adaptive systems.

struggling-young.description: Age 6, low ability (50-60% even at grade 0), tests system behavior with most challenged learner
struggling-young.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 0 frontier. Minimal mastery growth possible. Heavy remediation. System must provide scaffolding without frustrating the student. Primary presentation. Tests absolute floor of the adaptive system.

underconfident.description: Age 10, average ability with consistently low confidence, tests confidence-system interaction
underconfident.behavioral_signature: Same ability curve as overconfident but opposite confidence. Placed at grade 2-3 frontier. Average mastery progression. Low confidence may cause unnecessary hint-seeking. Should NOT be penalized for low confidence when answers are correct. Slower difficulty convergence due to hesitant behavior.

struggling-middle.description: Age 10, below-grade-level ability (55-65% at K-1), tests struggling without age extremes
struggling-middle.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 1 frontier. Very slow mastery growth similar to other struggling profiles but without age-related confounds. Intermediate presentation (age 10). Heavy remediation. Tests floor behavior in a mid-age learner.

fast-learner-older.description: Age 13, starts average but ability increases +0.5% per session, tests improving ability with standard presentation
fast-learner-older.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 3 frontier. Should show accelerating mastery as ability curve shifts up. Presentation stays standard (age 13). Difficulty targeting should track the improving ability over time. Eventually reaches higher-grade topics.

multi-misconception.description: Age 11, struggles in fractions AND place-value simultaneously, tests multi-strand remediation
multi-misconception.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 4 frontier (high general ability). Rapid mastery of non-misconception topics. Heavy remediation on both fraction AND place-value topics (8 misconception targets). Should trigger remediation chains in multiple strands. Tests whether remediation routing handles concurrent multi-strand issues.

irregular-attendance.description: Age 10, average ability, sessions every 2-5 days, tests FSRS interval robustness
irregular-attendance.behavioral_signature: Same ability as overconfident/underconfident but with irregular scheduling. Placed at grade 2-3 frontier. Mastery growth should be slower due to longer inter-session gaps causing more forgetting. FSRS intervals must handle variable gaps correctly. Review queue may be larger after multi-day gaps.

returning-after-gap.description: Age 12, average ability, 30 sessions then 14-day break then 30 more, tests mastery retention
returning-after-gap.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 3 frontier. Normal mastery growth for first 30 sessions. After 14-day gap, mastery should dip (FSRS decay) then recover within 5-10 sessions. Tests gap resilience — how many sessions to recover pre-gap mastery level. Review queue should spike after gap.

strong-highschool.description: Age 16, high ability (92%+) across all grades, tests advanced presentation with older student
strong-highschool.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 5 frontier (max). Near-100% mastery from diagnostic. Minimal mastery growth needed. No remediation. Advanced presentation (age 16). Tests system ceiling behavior with oldest supported age. Sessions complete quickly due to content ceiling — final mastery may be lower than ability suggests due to early exits.

average-undergraduate.description: Age 20, adult revisiting foundations, moderate ability K-3 with drop-off grade 4+
average-undergraduate.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 3 frontier. Steady mastery of K-3 topics, struggles at grade 4+. Advanced presentation (age 20). Tests that presentation adapts to adult vocabulary and engagement style without changing analytical level. Moderate remediation at grade boundary.

adult-remedial.description: Age 35, adult with low ability re-learning basics, tests advanced presentation + slow mastery
adult-remedial.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 1 frontier. Slow mastery growth — adult re-learning fundamentals. Advanced presentation (age 35) despite low ability — system must present basic content in adult-appropriate language. Heavy remediation. Tests the most extreme age-ability mismatch.

gifted-middle.description: Age 10, very high ability K-5 (95%+), tests system at content ceiling mid-age range
gifted-middle.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 5 frontier (max). Near-100% mastery from diagnostic. Minimal growth needed. No remediation. Presentation starts intermediate (age 10) but high ability across grades 0-5 pushes it up to standard. Tests ceiling behavior with younger student who has maxed out K-5 content.

slow-steady.description: Age 9, low learning gain but consistent daily attendance, tests patience of adaptive systems
slow-steady.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 1-2 frontier. Very slow but consistent mastery growth (+0.2% ability per session). Intermediate presentation (age 9). Tests whether the system maintains engagement over many sessions without noticeable progress. Should still show positive mastery trend over 30+ sessions.

binge-learner.description: Age 11, average ability, practices in bursts of 3-4 sessions/day then 2-3 day gaps
binge-learner.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 3 frontier. Clustered sessions mean FSRS sees multiple reviews in rapid succession (same-day intervals) followed by multi-day gaps. Mastery growth should be comparable to daily but more erratic — rapid progress during bursts, slight decay during gaps. Tests whether FSRS handles sub-day intervals correctly and whether review queue management works with bursty patterns.

weekend-warrior.description: Age 13, average ability, only practices on weekends (2 days on, 5 off)
weekend-warrior.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 3 frontier. Weekly 5-day gaps mean FSRS stability must survive regular breaks. Mastery growth should be significantly slower than daily — each weekend must re-review lapsed topics before progressing. Review queue should spike each Saturday. Tests whether the system prioritizes retention recovery over new topic introduction after gaps.

fading-motivation.description: Age 10, average ability, starts daily then gradually spaces out to every 3-5 days
fading-motivation.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 2-3 frontier. Good early mastery growth (daily sessions) that slows as intervals increase. By session 20+, intervals are 3-5 days — FSRS must handle the transition from short to long intervals. Mastery may plateau or decline as gaps exceed FSRS-predicted intervals. Tests progressive disengagement — the most realistic 'at-risk' usage pattern.

completion-driven.description: Age 12, above-average ability, daily until strand mastery then 3-7 day break
completion-driven.behavioral_signature: Placed at grade 3-4 frontier. Steady mastery growth during active periods. After mastering a strand/cluster, takes a break — tests whether mastered topics retain stability across the gap and whether the system correctly resumes with the next strand. Should show staircase mastery curve (climb, plateau, climb). Higher overall mastery than daily peers due to focused intent.

multi-math-strong.description: Age 14, strong math K-8, control for multi-subject math expansion
multi-math-strong.behavioral_signature: Runs diagnostic on both math-foundations and math-middle. Placed at grade 5 frontier in foundations, grade 6-7 in middle. Sessions draw from both subjects. Should show continued progression into grade 6-8 content without frontier exhaustion. Presentation drifts to advanced.

multi-average.description: Age 11, average ability across math + ELA, tests multi-subject session mix
multi-average.behavioral_signature: Diagnostics on math-foundations and ela-k5. Sessions interleave topics from both subjects. Should show parallel mastery growth in both subjects. ELA topics at lower grades should progress quickly. Standard presentation.

multi-strong-math-weak-ela.description: Age 10, strong math but weak ELA, tests cross-discipline prerequisite blocking
multi-strong-math-weak-ela.behavioral_signature: Strong in math (90%+), weak in ELA (60% at grade 0). ELA diagnostic places at grade 0-1. Math word problem topics gated by ELA reading prerequisites should remain blocked until ELA mastery is established. Should show asymmetric mastery: high math, low ELA.

multi-history-focus.description: Age 12, ELA + US History, tests context-layered discipline progression
multi-history-focus.behavioral_signature: Diagnostics on ela-k5 and us-history. History uses context-layered model: breadth-first at survey depth, then contextual depth. ELA provides reading comprehension prerequisites for history primary source analysis. Should show history topics progressing breadth-first before depth spiral.

multi-all-subjects.description: Age 13, all 4 subjects, tests full cross-discipline integration and session diversity
multi-all-subjects.behavioral_signature: Diagnostics on all 4 subjects. Sessions draw from math, ELA, and history. Should show diverse topic selection across subjects. Cross-discipline prerequisites respected. Mastery spread across all subjects rather than deep in one. Standard presentation.
